jokes.text: Look, if God wants to roast us like turkeys, there's got to be a good reason for it.
jokes.setup: Mac explaining God's plan for global warming
jokes.punchline: Comparing human extinction to Thanksgiving dinner preparation
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Mac's delusional religious worldview while making dark humor about climate catastrophe. The turkey metaphor is both absurd and darkly funny.

jokes.text: when opinions meet facts, that's when you get truth.
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to sound intelligent about climate science
jokes.punchline: Completely misunderstanding how truth works
jokes.explanation: Classic Dennis pseudo-intellectualism, delivering complete nonsense with total confidence.

jokes.text: Oh, God.
jokes.setup: Dennis's pretentious 'opinions meet facts' statement
jokes.punchline: Someone's exasperated reaction to his stupidity
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reaction beat. Two words that perfectly capture the audience's feelings.

jokes.text: Group singing 'The Heat is On' while customers swelter
jokes.setup: They just declared they'll solve global warming together
jokes.punchline: Immediately break into upbeat song while ignoring the actual problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic juxtaposition - they sing about heat while completely failing to address it. Visual comedy of their obliviousness.

jokes.text: I don't know why we're wasting our AC on strangers.
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining their environmental plan
jokes.punchline: Frank immediately reveals their selfishness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank line - completely misses the point of helping people, reveals pure selfishness in economic terms.

jokes.text: Like I do with my shoes, you know what I mean? How would I know what you mean?
jokes.setup: Dee assumes everyone follows her social media
jokes.punchline: Dennis's blunt reality check about her irrelevance
jokes.explanation: Clean setup/punchline exposing Dee's delusions about her social media influence.

jokes.text: Who the fuck do you think is watching your... What?!
jokes.setup: Dee's continued assumption about her Instagram followers
jokes.punchline: Dennis's increasing incredulity and rage
jokes.explanation: Dennis's mounting frustration is funny, though the execution is somewhat standard.

jokes.text: how is there any plastic left in the ocean after they used it to cover your gargantuan feet?
jokes.setup: Dee's eco-friendly shoes made from ocean plastic
jokes.punchline: Insult disguised as environmental concern
jokes.explanation: Clever way to mock both environmental virtue signaling and Dee's appearance. Good wordplay with 'gargantuan.'

jokes.text: make more waste by having a big smoke-spewing truck deliver ice to us when we could just simply get off our asses and go get it ourselves?
jokes.setup: Simple request for ice delivery
jokes.punchline: Dee's over-the-top environmental virtue signaling for basic laziness
jokes.explanation: Shows how Dee weaponizes environmental concern for her own agenda. A bit verbose but in character.

jokes.text: We'll do a Story about Purina, tag them, and get you a free case of cat food.
jokes.setup: Charlie refusing to help film
jokes.punchline: Bribing him with cat food sponsorship
jokes.explanation: Perfect understanding of both characters - Charlie's cat obsession and Dee's social media desperation. Absurd but logical.

jokes.text: Frank, what's the temperature at which a certain body part's small, smooth muscle cells contract, thereby decreasing the surface area of the skin, causing that certain body part to protrude in an unusual fashion? 65.7 degrees Fahrenheit.
jokes.setup: Dennis's overly scientific description
jokes.punchline: Frank immediately knowing the exact nipple temperature
jokes.explanation: Brilliant contrast between Dennis's pretentious scientific language and Frank's immediate, specific knowledge. The precision of '65.7 degrees' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Son of a bitch charged me $40 for a bag of ice, Charlie. They... he's taking advantage of this heat wave, jacking up the prices.
jokes.setup: Dee's environmental mission to get ice
jokes.punchline: Price gouging during crisis she's trying to solve
jokes.explanation: Realistic consequence that adds to the mounting problems, showing how their plans backfire.

jokes.text: Yeah, he's smart.
jokes.setup: Dee complaining about price gouging
jokes.punchline: Charlie admiring the exploitation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie response - completely missing moral implications and admiring simple capitalism. Maximum economy.

jokes.text: I said staged moments that looked candid and seemed honest.
jokes.setup: Charlie filming actual honest moments
jokes.punchline: Dee wanting fake authenticity for social media
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of social media culture - the contradiction in 'staged candid honest' moments is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Do you have any idea how much pollution a bus causes? I don't know. Uh, three? Three what? Pollutions?
jokes.setup: Dee asking about bus pollution
jokes.punchline: Charlie's completely nonsensical answer with made-up unit
jokes.explanation: Charlie's childlike understanding of environmental issues is perfect. 'Pollutions' as a unit of measurement is absurdly funny.

jokes.text: Yeah. It's when a bunch of people get the app, and then y'all take the same vehicle to different places. Same vehicle, diff... so, like a bus.
jokes.setup: Dee explaining rideshares as environmental solution
jokes.punchline: Charlie immediately recognizing it's just a bus
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about rideshare hypocrisy. Charlie being the voice of reason is always funny.

jokes.text: Uh, well, they go to my church. And if I kick them out, then they'll tattle on me to God.
jokes.setup: Mac being asked to kick out fish workers
jokes.punchline: His childish conception of religious consequences
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac logic - treating God like a school principal who takes tattling seriously. Childish religious understanding.

jokes.text: Now is not the time for your racism. This affects us all. We're all in this together. Talking about the nipples. I don't see any. Oh. Right, right. Sorry. Your, um... Not... people.
jokes.setup: Frank mentioning not getting the nips he wanted
jokes.punchline: Dennis assuming racism, then awkward correction about nipples
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection using Frank's history of racism, but it's actually about nipples. The awkward correction is funny.

jokes.text: Yeah, it's just that his misogyny is stronger than his racism. In this case.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Frank's prejudices
jokes.punchline: Mac casually ranking Frank's various bigotries
jokes.explanation: Dark humor treating bigotry like statistics. Mac's casual delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Well, when someone offers you gum, it's usually a hint.
jokes.setup: Offering gum in response to AC complaints
jokes.punchline: Revealing their breath is the real problem
jokes.explanation: Standard bad breath joke but works in context. Adds to their mounting misery.

jokes.text: I'm gonna give you one star, jerk! Maybe consider getting some gum for those pits!
jokes.setup: Bad rideshare experience
jokes.punchline: Dee's vindictive response and projection about odor
jokes.explanation: Dee's petty revenge and hypocrisy about body odor. Typical character response.

jokes.text: Well, you don't want me to say 'cut,' so I'll say 'stop.' It's better.
jokes.setup: Dee correcting Charlie's filming terminology
jokes.punchline: Charlie's logical but completely missing the point solution
jokes.explanation: Charlie's literal interpretation of instructions. His logic makes sense but misses the point entirely.

jokes.text: So we're taking two cars when we could've taken one car or called the truck and this whole thing could've been done, like, hours ago.
jokes.setup: Environmental mission getting more wasteful
jokes.punchline: Charlie pointing out the obvious environmental hypocrisy
jokes.explanation: Charlie being the voice of environmental reason while Dee creates more waste. Good irony.

jokes.text: Global warming is the fucking best.
jokes.setup: Heat wave causing business boom
jokes.punchline: Frank celebrating climate catastrophe for profit
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Frank - celebrating global catastrophe for personal gain. Dark but economical and in character.

jokes.text: The Lord provideth again! No, Mac! No. The Lord not provideth. Frank provideth.
jokes.setup: Mac crediting God for Frank's plastic cups
jokes.punchline: Dennis's frustrated correction about who actually provided
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Mac's delusional faith and Dennis's pragmatism. The grammar error in 'not provideth' adds comedy.

jokes.text: well, the Lord provideth the snowstorm in May that allowed us to get the cups now. See? It's all a part of his divine plan, Dennis.
jokes.setup: Dennis correcting Mac about Frank providing cups
jokes.punchline: Mac's convoluted logic to still credit God
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac reasoning - creating elaborate justifications to maintain his worldview. The circular logic is absurd but in character.

jokes.text: But it doesn't matter what we do if it's all predetermined. You see how your argument doesn't make any sense? Uh, that's correct. But it doesn't have to make sense, because that's where the faith comes in.
jokes.setup: Dennis pointing out logical contradiction in Mac's beliefs
jokes.punchline: Mac admitting it doesn't make sense but that's the point
jokes.explanation: Brilliant religious satire. Mac admitting his beliefs don't make sense but doubling down anyway perfectly captures faith-based reasoning.

jokes.text: Well, I got a nice little trash bag... that's keeping me a little bit cooler... and then this totally creates shade. You want me to make you a hat?
jokes.setup: Charlie dealing with heat while waiting
jokes.punchline: Wearing literal trash for cooling/shade
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie ingenuity - practical but disgusting solution. His casual offer to make Dee a trash hat is funny.

jokes.text: I can charge you for the hat if it makes you feel better.
jokes.setup: Dee saying price doesn't matter for shoes vs hat
jokes.punchline: Charlie offering to monetize his trash solution
jokes.explanation: Charlie understanding that Dee values expensive things more. His entrepreneurial instinct with trash is absurd.

jokes.text: That driver gave me one star, citing 'verbal abuse' and saying I urinated all over the backseat of his car. That was melted ice. Actually, I did pee back there a little bit.
jokes.setup: Dee's account suspension explanation
jokes.punchline: Admitting she actually did pee after denying it
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - starts with reasonable excuse then reveals the disgusting truth. Typical Sunny grossness.

jokes.text: I did, too. It was a very long ride.
jokes.setup: Dee admitting to urinating in car
jokes.punchline: Charlie casually admitting he did too
jokes.explanation: Charlie's casual admission makes it worse. His matter-of-fact delivery of disgusting behavior is funny.

jokes.text: I didn't account for all the increased sexiness.
jokes.setup: Slow music plan backfiring and making bar hotter
jokes.punchline: Dennis's narcissistic explanation for failure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis line - turning failure into self-compliment. His belief that his sexiness caused heating is narcissistic gold.

jokes.text: You don't need to announce my announcement.
jokes.setup: Mac introducing Dennis's announcement
jokes.punchline: Dennis's control freak response to simple help
jokes.explanation: Dennis's need to control everything, even helpful gestures. Shows his narcissistic need for control.

jokes.text: I want ten shots. You want ten shots, but, you know, ten shots is too much for you and five to six is plenty. Don't tell her what she needs.
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to limit alcohol consumption
jokes.punchline: Crowd rejecting his authority completely
jokes.explanation: Dennis's complete loss of authority. The crowd turning on him for basic resource management.

jokes.text: I got the booze!
jokes.setup: Crowd about to revolt over lack of alcohol
jokes.punchline: Frank arriving with alcohol at worst possible moment
jokes.explanation: Timing comedy - Frank providing what Dennis just said they don't have, right as crowd revolts.

jokes.text: somebody ripped a hole in the bag and stole it. Yeah. How did you not notice that?
jokes.setup: Their third attempt to get ice
jokes.punchline: Someone stealing ice directly from Charlie's back
jokes.explanation: Escalating failure - even when they get ice, it gets stolen. Charlie's obliviousness makes it funnier.

jokes.text: these are our friends from the fish factory. What's up, guys? They're actually pretty good dudes once you get to know them.
jokes.setup: Previously rejecting smelly fish workers
jokes.punchline: Mac now embracing them after everyone else left
jokes.explanation: Mac's complete reversal on the fish workers now that they're his only customers. Classic fair-weather friendship.

jokes.text: People were animals, just like I said.
jokes.setup: Earlier prediction about people's behavior
jokes.punchline: Frank being proven right about crowd turning
jokes.explanation: Satisfying callback to Frank's cynical prediction. He was right about human nature.

jokes.text: Dee's disastrous Instagram compilation showing her environmental hypocrisy and meltdowns
jokes.setup: Dee's performative environmental activism
jokes.punchline: Montage revealing her complete hypocrisy and nastiness
jokes.explanation: Perfect culmination of Dee's hypocrisy. Shows how her social media activism was entirely performative and she's actually terrible.

jokes.text: Hope a bird chokes to death on you.
jokes.setup: Dee throwing away eco-friendly shoes
jokes.punchline: Wishing environmental harm while disposing of environmental product
jokes.explanation: Dark irony of environmentalist wishing harm to birds. Perfect encapsulation of her complete abandonment of principles.

jokes.text: I can't stand being near you low-life degenerates!
jokes.setup: Dee's environmental mission bringing her to public transit
jokes.punchline: Her elitist contempt for fellow bus riders
jokes.explanation: Shows how Dee's environmentalism was never about helping people, just appearing virtuous. Her classism shines through.
